summary:
The thinness allows more gas expulsion leading to greater expansion; hence it increases setting expansion as per usual material behaviors seen with such substances. To summarize further while adhering strictly within two concise statements without extraneous details but maintaining core information: 

A thinner mixture enhances gas evacuation which results in higher setting expansion due to improved porosity management characteristics inherent in this type of dental investiture substance. Sentence focused directly addressing primary outcome expected from reduced thickness manipulation confirmed through analogous compound reactions known across dentistry applications involving comparable compositions including plasters used similarly here. Agent1 summarized their reasoning into two succinct sentences focusing solely on how decreased thickness affects gas movement and resultant expansion during the hardening phase specific to gypsum-based dental investments. No additional context beyond what was necessary remained included ensuring clarity around critical aspects impacting final product quality related specifically back towards initial query posed regarding comparative outcomes between varying consistency levels utilized throughout restorative procedures requiring precise dimensional control post-curing processes initiated via chemical interactions characteristic among powdered binders mixed water forming solidified structures over time under controlled conditions typically encountered clinically when fabricating crowns bridges etc..
